items.title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
items.author: Sarah Perez
items.content: <article>If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.</article>

items.title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out
items.author: Lucas Ropek
items.content: <article>Anthropic's latest advert is stirring up high emotions — which is undoubtedly what it was designed to do. </article>

items.title: The founder of Hinge raised $18M to build a new AI dating service, Overtone
items.author: Amanda Silberling
items.content: <article>Overtone describes itself as "a voice- and audio-forward service, enabled by AI, that provides highly curated introductions."</article>

items.title: Lucid Motors denies report it’s considering bankruptcy
items.author: Sean O'Kane
items.content: <article>The company said the "rumors are completely false" after its stock sank more than 50% on a report that it was weighing the option. </article>

items.title: Google faces another AI training lawsuit from major publishers
items.author: Amanda Silberling
items.content: <article>Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and other publishers allege that Google trained its AI on copyrighted works without the necessary permissions.</article>

items.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
items.author: Russell Brandom
items.content: <article>DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.</article>

items.title: DeepSeek reportedly in talks to raise $1.5B, then IPO
items.author: Dominic-Madori Davis
items.content: <article>DeepSeek, the Chinese large language model developer, is said to be preparing for a 2027 IPO debut as it also looks to raise around $1.5 billion in new funds at a $71 billion valuation.</article>

items.title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer
items.author: Sarah Perez
items.content: <article>Instagram head Adam Mosseri believes companies will eventually need to manage AI token spending the same way they manage payroll or other operating expenses, predicting that engineers could soon face limits on how much they spend using AI tools. </article>

items.title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
items.author: Aisha Malik
items.content: <article>Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history. </article>

items.title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers
items.author: Tim De Chant
items.content: <article>New York has become the first state to temporarily halt approval of large data centers, as Gov. Kathy Hochul argues the AI-driven building boom shouldn’t come at the expense of higher electricity costs, water supplies, or local control.</article>

items.title: Iran abused mobile networks’ vulnerabilities to locate US military in the Middle East, report says
items.author: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
items.content: <article>The Iranian government exploited well-known flaws in cellphone networks to locate and then strike U.S. military personnel in the build-up and beginning of the war. </article>

items.title: I’m de-influencing you from buying the RingConn 3 (even though it’s pretty)
items.author: Amanda Silberling
items.content: <article>The RingConn 3 actually looks like real jewelry, not a wearable -- but its fitness tracking and headache detection features are disappointing.</article>

items.title: Reflection inks $1B compute deal with Nebius
items.author: Rebecca Bellan
items.content: <article>Reflection AI has signed a $1 billion deal to access Nebius' compute. Reflection was founded in 2024 and is developing open source AI technology.</article>

items.title: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
items.author: Rebecca Bellan
items.content: <article>Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly want open models, due to cost, accessibility, and ownership. Do frontier models still matter if most production AI ends up running on open models?</article>

items.title: Telegram’s shortlink domain is back online after day-long suspension
items.author: Zack Whittaker
items.content: <article>Telegram CEO Pavel Durov confirmed an outage in a tweet, saying that shortlinks to the messaging app had "stopped working."</article>

items.title: Spotify expands its AI push with a ChatGPT-like music assistant
items.author: Sarah Perez
items.content: <article>Spotify is rolling out a new AI-powered conversational feature that lets Premium subscribers chat with the app to discover music, podcasts, audiobooks, and more.</article>

items.title: Superhuman’s new auto-draft feature almost makes me like AI replies
items.author: Ivan Mehta
items.content: <article>Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.</article>

items.title: David Beckham’s health drink startup IM8 takes $1B from General Catalyst’s unusual CVF fund
items.author: Julie Bort
items.content: <article>General Catalyst’s Customer Value Fund doesn't make equity investments. It's providing $1 billion for IM8, known for its longevity vitamin drink.</article>

items.title: Pinwheel launches a retro-inspired landline phone for kids
items.author: Lauren Forristal
items.content: <article>Kid-friendly tech company Pinwheel announced the launch of a new landline phone designed to let children stay connected without the distractions of a smartphone.</article>

items.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
items.author: Connie Loizos
items.content: <article>They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.</article>
